Here’s a recap of yesterday’s transfer rumours.

NICOLAS PEPE:
• Mohamed Bouhafsi is back at it again, claiming the Reds want to do a deal this summer - however Liverpool are growing anxious at having not heard anything while he’s away on Africa Cup of Nations duty.
• As echoed by the likes of Dom King and the Echo, these reports are categorically not true.

RYAN KENT:
• Rangers have reached an agreement to re-sign Ryan Kent this summer says Football Insider, but only if LFC allow the winger to depart on loan again.
• Rangers are favourites to acquire Kent’s services this summer, however given the reliability of the source, I’m not sure a deal has been agreed as of yet.

PHILIPPE COUTINHO:
• Liverpool are considering a move for Philippe Coutinho according to the latest reports in Spain.
• According to Sport, Coutinho is favouring a move to the Premier League and is keen on returning to Anfield.
• Nonsense report - Liverpool have no interest in bringing Phil back.

SIMON MIGNOLET:
• Simon Mignolet is reportedly keen to leave Liverpool in search of first team football says the Evening Standard.
• His representatives are said to be aware how much it would take for the Reds to consider selling the goalkeeper, with the figure claimed to be more than the £8m bid Crystal Palace are believed to be preparing.
• Liverpool seem to be happy to keep Mignolet this summer, however will sell if the player wants to leave and the price is right.

BOBBY DUNCAN:
• The Daily Mail are reporting a number of foreign clubs, including Borussia Dortmund, are keeping close tabs on Duncan, who has less than two years left on his current deal.
• The Mail claim the teenager will look to quit Anfield if he’s not in Klopp’s plans this season.
• At the minute, I highly doubt boyhood Reds fan Duncan is considering leaving a season after signing. I believe he’ll have his chances this season.

DANI CEBALLOS:
• Mauricio Pochettino has urged Tottenham to sign Dani Ceballos from Real Madrid, having become wary of LFC’s rumoured interest in the Real Madrid star, says Sport.
• These reports about Klopp being interested in the Spain U21 star seem very much fabricated. 🔴 #LFC

對於今次出書，阿旦今日接受《蘋果》電話訪問時：「其實今次影嘅相集，真係無心插柳！因為同攝影師真係好老友，每次有新project或新idea，都會想搵佢影相。平時自己平台或者製作一個節目嘅版面，可能只需要用一、兩張相就OK！不過影完輯相出嚟，發覺仲有好多相都好靚，唔share出嚟又好浪費，所以攝影師就提議用我嘅相結集成相集！好似《私圖》嘅封面，係我生日嘅時候同朋友去石垣島玩時影嘅靚相。之前都有好多人叫我出書，但我本身其實冇諗過再出任何相集，但如果今次反應好，我又可以再考慮吓。」

Here’s an overview of all of Saturday’s Liverpool transfer gossip.

RYAN KENT:
• It appears Ryan Kent may not be on the move this summer after all.
• According to the Mirror, Jürgen Klopp has blocked loan approaches from Rangers and Leeds as he wants the winger to play a backup role next season.
• I am surprised by this, but definitely not disappointed. Ryan is no doubt a talent and I will be very happy to see him in red next season.

DANI OLMO:
• Liverpool have been linked to Spainish U21 sensation Dani Olmo by Mundo Deportivo.
• They say the Reds, along with Tottenham, have been handed a major boost in their pursuit of the Dinamo Zagreb star.
• This comes after his agent reportedly told the newspaper he isn’t keen on joining a La Liga side, amidst interest from Atletico Madrid, Barcelona and Valencia, and that Merseyside, north London or even Munich were the most viable destinations for the ace this summer.

PHILIPPE COUTINHO:
• Phil’s agent has spoken out today, revealing he was told Barcelona “have no intention of selling him”, which is very much contrary to reports coming from Spain.
• It’s very unlikely the Reds would be interested either way, but maybe our former Little Magician isn’t on the move after all.

NABIL FEKIR:
• Could Nabil finally be on his way? Le Parisien certainly think so, reporting Fekir has agreed terms with Real Betis one year after his failed transfer to Liverpool.
• This does come after Jean-Michel Aulas claimed Lyon is ready to offer the Frenchman a new contract, throwing the accuracy of the report into question.
• This doesn’t really concern the Reds - it’s just a bit fascinating to see what’s going on with the man who came so close to joining us 12 months ago. 🔴 #LFC
